chapter_title.ar: باب أَخْذِ الْأُجْرَةِ وَ الرِّشَا عَلَى الْحُكْمِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 5 – Taking the recompense and the bribery upon the judgement

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/269)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (24/269)
text: سُئِلَ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) عَنْ قَاضٍ بَيْنَ قَرْيَتَيْنِ يَأْخُذُ مِنَ السُّلْطَانِ عَلَى الْقَضَاءِ الرِّزْقَ فَقَالَ ذَلِكَ السُّحْتُ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Mahboub, from Abdullah Bin Sinan who said,

‘Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> was asked about a judge between two towns taking the livelihood from the ruling authorities upon the judgements. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘That is the ill-gotten (forbidden)’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn Mahbub from ‘ Abd Allah ibn Sinan who has narrated the following: “Once abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, was asked about a judge between two towns who receives his wages from al-Sultan. He (the Imam) said, ‘It is a sinful earning.”’
